key_insight: The paper turns a Fokker-Planck-type elliptic PDE into a symmetric positive definite least-squares weak Galerkin system, enabling stable finite element solves even with non-smooth diffusion tensors.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for robust PDE simulation workflows where solver stability, mesh-based discretization flexibility, and provable error control matter.
method: Uses a least-squares formulation within the weak Galerkin framework.
Constructs locally defined weak second-order partial derivatives and weak divergence operators.
Proves uniqueness and optimal-order error estimates in a discrete energy norm.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive numerical experiments that validate the theory and demonstrate robustness and performance.
watch_item: The method’s practicality may depend on careful implementation of the weak operators and on the cost of assembling and solving the resulting SPD system.
paper_title: A Least Squares Weak Galerkin Finite Element Method for Fokker-Planck Type Equations
